idea-plugin.name: Engram
idea-plugin.description:
Engram is a durable, graph-based long-term memory for AI coding assistants — the reasoning/decision layer (why we chose this, what bit us, what's still open), shown as a graph you can see, edit, and own.

This plugin embeds the Engram graph pane in a tool window. It connects to the local engram serve daemon — the same one your AI assistant reads from and writes to over MCP — so decisions, cautions, problems, and insights surface and update live as you work.

One memory, every agent: the backend speaks MCP, so Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Kilo, and Google Antigravity all share the same per-repo graph — a decision captured by one assistant is recalled by the next.

Requires the Engram backend. Install it and run engram serve in your repository; the pane connects automatically once the daemon is up.

Pairs naturally with projects that keep their canon in PLAN.md and compile decision logs to DECISIONS.md — Engram is the living graph those documents are rendered from.
